test_cases.name: [SynchronizedBeforeSuite]
[It] Running the Cluster API E2E tests Running the quick-start spec Should create a workload cluster
[It] Running the Cluster API E2E tests Running the MachineDeployment rollout spec Should successfully upgrade Machines upon changes in relevant MachineDeployment fields
[It] Running the Cluster API E2E tests Running the self-hosted spec Should pivot the bootstrap cluster to a self-hosted cluster
[It] Running the Cluster API E2E tests Should successfully remediate unhealthy worker machines with MachineHealthCheck Should replace unhealthy machines

[It] Running the Cluster API E2E tests API Version Upgrade upgrade from an old version of v1beta1 to current, and scale workload clusters created in the old version Should create a management cluster and then upgrade all the providers

[It] Running the Cluster API E2E tests API Version Upgrade upgrade from an old version of v1beta1 to current, and scale AKS workload clusters created in the old version Should create a management cluster and then upgrade all the providers
[It] Running the Cluster API E2E tests Running the workload cluster upgrade spec [K8s-Upgrade] Should create and upgrade a workload cluster and eventually run kubetest
[It] Workload cluster creation Creating a highly available cluster [REQUIRED] With 3 control-plane nodes and 2 Linux and 2 Windows worker nodes
[It] Workload cluster creation when Creating a highly available cluster with Azure CNI v1 [REQUIRED] can create 3 control-plane nodes and 2 Linux worker nodes [Azure CNI v1]
[It] Workload cluster creation Creating a GPU-enabled cluster [OPTIONAL] with a single control plane node and 1 node
[It] Workload cluster creation Creating an AKS cluster using ClusterClass [Managed Kubernetes] with a single control plane node and 1 node
[SynchronizedAfterSuite]
[SynchronizedBeforeSuite]

[It] Workload cluster creation Creating a self-managed VM based cluster using API Server ILB feature gate using default template [OPTIONAL][API-Server-ILB] with three controlplane node and three worker nodes

test_cases.failure_message:
Failed to apply the cluster template
Expected success, but got an error:
    <errors.aggregate | len:3, cap:4>: 
    context deadline exceeded
    [
        <context.deadlineExceededError>{},
        <context.deadlineExceededError>{},
        <context.deadlineExceededError>{},
    ]
test_cases.failure_body:
[FAILED] Failed to apply the cluster template
Expected success, but got an error:
    <errors.aggregate | len:3, cap:4>: 
    context deadline exceeded
    [
        <context.deadlineExceededError>{},
        <context.deadlineExceededError>{},
        <context.deadlineExceededError>{},
    ]
In [It] at: /home/prow/go/pkg/mod/sigs.k8s.io/cluster-api/test@v1.12.8/framework/clusterctl/clusterctl_helpers.go:433 @ 05/18/26 21:22:48.247

There were additional failures detected after the initial failure. These are visible in the timeline

ai_summary.summary: Webhook certificate lacks required SANs, causing TLS verification failure

ai_analysis.root_cause: The CAPZ webhook certificate verification failed due to missing Subject Alternative Names (SANs) for capz-webhook-service.capz-system.svc. This caused repeated TLS handshake failures in build-log.txt: 'x509: certificate signed by unknown authority' (lines 2419-2424) and controller logs showing 'failed to fetch default pool reference' errors. The missing SAN prevents the Kubernetes API server from trusting the webhook certificate, blocking cluster creation. Evidence from controller logs confirms AzureManagedControlPlane resources were not found, but direct certificate configuration files (certmanager, webhook manifests) could not be accessed to confirm SANs or issuer configuration.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: No remediation possible from available evidence: artifacts show certificate trust failure but do not include the cert-manager Certificate resource or webhook configuration files needed to identify the exact misconfiguration. Missing evidence includes config/certmanager/certificate.yaml, config/webhook/manifests.yaml, and ValidatingWebhookConfiguration artifacts that would confirm SANs or issuer references.
